_meta.note: Seed registry. Speed/VRAM marked low/medium confidence are inferred from non-5090 hardware; on-device 5090 measurement pending. Prices verified against primary sources where a URL is given.
_meta.$per_min_4K_definition: USD per minute of 720p->4K@24fps output, extrapolated from a 5s clip

tools.name: SeedVR2 (3B / 7B)

speed.unit: fps MEASURED (7B fp8 1080p batch5+offload=0.35; 3B fp8 1080p batch9=0.11; 3B fp8 4K batch1 tiled=0.13)

tools.ai_gen_note: Strongest detail rebuild, but its own model card warns it over-sharpens clean 720p AI video. Lower restoration strength.
tools.use_when: Local cinema quality-max on a 5090; AI footage needing genuine detail rebuild; Apache-2.0 reproducibility.
tools.avoid_when: Real-time throughput; already-clean footage where a faithful 2x is enough (it over-cooks).
tools.notes: ICLR 2026 (repo-claimed). The 'SideVR' the brief half-remembered. MEASURED on the 5090: throughput is dominated by VAE-decode batching, not model size; batch 9 maxes 32GB at 1080p and OOMs at 4K; efficient path = small batch + VAE tiling + block-swap with --dit_offload_device cpu. SageAttention 3 active. See data/measurements-5090.json.

tools.name: FlashVSR
tools.vendor: OpenImagingLab / Shanghai AI Lab (built on Alibaba Wan2.1)
tools.license: GPL-3.0 (ComfyUI wrappers); Wan2.1 base weights own terms (verify)

speed.unit: fps MEASURED (tiny x2 720p->1440p, 25fr, ~31GB); x4/4K blocked on 32GB (OOM + repo bugs)

tools.ai_gen_note: Prior is the text-to-video domain; streaming design fights AI flicker; conservative, invents less than SeedVR2.
tools.use_when: Dual-use: 4K auteur pass (Full + tiling) and short-drama volume (Tiny). Diffusion quality at near real-time.
tools.avoid_when: You need a non-GPL redistributable pipeline, or max plausible detail rebuild (SeedVR2 wins).
tools.notes: MEASURED on the 5090 (FlashVSR_plus build, isolated venv torch 2.9): tiny x2 = 0.80 fps near the 32GB ceiling; x4/4K BLOCKED (OOM without tiling; tiled-dit/full hit repo bug UnboundLocalError temp_name). The A100 ~17 fps marketing does not hold here. Needs >=21 input frames (streaming window).

tools.name: Topaz Video, Proteus (Manual)

speed.unit: fps MEASURED 720p->1080p (7.3 @4K); peak VRAM ~1.8-2.7GB; steady-state via tvai ffmpeg CLI

tools.ai_gen_note: Hand-tuned, face-safe with gentle sliders, no hallucination. Push hard and you get halos + plastic skin.
tools.use_when: Face-safe, deterministic, zero-hallucination cleanup; local unlimited offline.
tools.avoid_when: You need detail rebuild beyond sharpening, or diffusion-grade texture.
tools.notes: App rebranded 'Topaz Video' (~v1.6.1), subscription-only since Oct 2025. MEASURED on the 5090 via the local tvai ffmpeg CLI (prob-4, -f null): 15 fps @1080p, 7.3 fps @4K, ~1.8-2.7GB VRAM. Runs headless offline from cached auth.tpz. Starlight diffusion needs Neuroserver (not via this CLI). See data/measurements-5090.json.

tools.name: Topaz Starlight Precise 2.5
tools.ai_gen_note: First Topaz diffusion video model with full temporal consistency; explicitly de-plastics synthetic faces/skin. Supersedes the old 'avoid Starlight on faces' rule for this model.
tools.use_when: Cinema hero shots needing de-plasticized faces; overnight render acceptable.
tools.avoid_when: Volume (0.2 fps wall), 8K, or interactive iteration.
tools.notes: Local vs cloud availability of Precise 2.5 reported inconsistently; Mini/Sharp are the practical local variants. Exact 5090 4K fps unverified.

tools.name: Topaz Astra (cloud)
models: Precision (Proteus, Starlight Precise 2.5, Rhea, Nyx, Iris)
tools.ai_gen_note: The only Topaz surface designed for GenAI footage (Sora/Veo/Kling/Seedance), scene-aware, Creative/Precision slider.
tools.use_when: Volume short-drama throughput without the local 0.2-fps wall; GenAI-native cleanup.
tools.avoid_when: Offline/privacy required, >4K, or frame-exact manual control.
tools.notes: 'Astra 2' generation referenced (May 2026 Expansion) but version string not consistently confirmed; pricing needs re-pull.

tools.name: WaveSpeed SeedVR2 (video API)
tools.ai_gen_note: SeedVR2 backend with flat per-second 4K pricing; cheapest verified 4K cloud path.
tools.use_when: Volume 4K where cost/throughput matters; one API for gen + upscale.
tools.avoid_when: You need a named festival-auditable license guarantee in writing.
tools.notes: Cheapest verified $/min of 4K in the API survey. Ownership/jurisdiction not confirmed.

tools.name: fal.ai Topaz Video Upscale
tools.ai_gen_note: Topaz house quality (Starlight family tuned for AI footage) without a subscription, watermark-free.
tools.use_when: Topaz quality per-clip; pick a model that adds detail without crisping plastic skin.
tools.avoid_when: Budget-critical at 4K (per-second 4K is the priciest tier; Gaia 2 halves it).
tools.notes: Replicate offers the same schedule. Exact Topaz model/version on the endpoint not stated.

tools.name: VEnhancer
tools.ai_gen_note: True space-time enhancement (spatial SR + frame interpolation + refinement), prompt-aware, built for text-to-video.
tools.use_when: You have A100/H100 access and want <=2K space-time enhancement of text-to-video.
tools.avoid_when: Only a 32 GB 5090 (out of spec, wants 80 GB), or you need 4K.
tools.notes: Frequently misattributed to Alibaba DAMO; it is Shanghai AI Lab.

tools.name: Real-ESRGAN
tools.vendor: Xintao Wang et al. (Tencent ARC origin)

speed.unit: fps (x4 full-frame 720p->2880p eager fp16; 0.88 fp32; anime_6B 5.3 fp16)

tools.ai_gen_note: Per-frame, no temporal model. Sharpens waxy skin and amplifies flicker on AI footage. Pair with vs_temporalfix for video.
tools.use_when: Cheap, fast, commercially-clean 4x; previews; anime variants on stylized content.
tools.avoid_when: Flickery or heavy-artifact synthetic photoreal where quality matters.
tools.notes: Speed/VRAM MEASURED on the reference RTX 5090 (NOMAD, torch 2.7+cu128); see data/measurements-5090.json. fp16 = 2x speed and half VRAM vs fp32.

tools.name: SUPIR
tools.vendor: Fanghua Yu, Jinjin Gu et al. (XPixel)
tools.license: Non-commercial (README overrides nominal MIT) + SDXL terms
tools.ai_gen_note: Best photoreal detail rebuild on a single soft AI still. v0F + low s_churn to stay faithful. No temporal lock (flickers on sequences).
tools.use_when: Max photoreal detail on a single AI hero still/keyframe; cinema/auteur (non-commercial license).
tools.avoid_when: Throughput, frame sequences, or a clean commercial license for the studio.
tools.notes: Needs PyTorch cu128 for Blackwell. 4090/5090 timings unanchored to a primary source.

tools.name: Clarity Upscaler
models: Juggernaut SD + ControlNet Tile + tiled diffusion
tools.ai_gen_note: Creative crispness bias; low creativity + high resemblance + tile<0.7 keeps it faithful, push creativity and it amplifies the AI look.
tools.use_when: Tunable, fast, local Magnific-style pass with a per-shot fidelity dial.
tools.avoid_when: Temporally stable frames, or AGPL/base-checkpoint licensing conflicts with a commercial deliverable.
tools.notes: Hosted as fal Clarity ($0.03/MP) and Replicate ($0.033/run); those are wrappers of this open model.

tools.name: Flux.1-dev ControlNet Upscaler (tile)
tools.ai_gen_note: Most controllable fidelity dial; cleanest, least-plastic skin of the tile options at low denoise.
tools.use_when: Faithful, top-fidelity tiling to any size with tight artifact control (cinema).
tools.avoid_when: Commercial deliverable without a BFL license (dev weights are non-commercial).
tools.notes: fp16 ~24GB fits 32GB; fp8/GGUF for big tiles.

tools.name: CCSR-v2
tools.ai_gen_note: Stable across noise seeds (where SUPIR/StableSR/SeeSR are not); cleans without aggressive re-invention, so it flickers least on frames.
tools.use_when: Per-frame consistency on AI-video frames matters more than max invented detail.
tools.avoid_when: You want SUPIR-level dramatic detail on a hero still.
tools.notes: Seed-stability is its differentiator for sequences.

tools.name: OSEDiff
tools.license: Apache-2.0 code; SD2.1-base weights own terms
tools.ai_gen_note: One-step, fast, faithful, low-invention; less flicker than multi-step SUPIR. Texture ceiling below SUPIR.
tools.use_when: Throughput/volume; fast faithful clean-up; per-frame work.
tools.avoid_when: SUPIR-grade invented microdetail on a hero still.
tools.notes: AdcSR distills this up to 9.3x faster (CVPR 2025).

tools.name: Real-CUGAN
tools.license: MIT (LICENSE is plain MIT, commercial OK)
tools.ai_gen_note: Best anime/2D line preservation with conservative + denoise dial; wrong tool for photoreal humans.
tools.use_when: Synthetic anime/illustration/2D footage; tunable denoise+upscale.
tools.avoid_when: Photoreal faces; confirmed commercial weight rights needed without checking.
tools.notes: Verification: LICENSE file is plain MIT (no commercial restriction); commercial OK.

tools.name: HAT
tools.ai_gen_note: Highest fidelity of the deterministic family; faithful, no invention; GAN variants add real texture. Slow.
tools.use_when: Quality-max within deterministic, commercial-clean (Apache), low volume cinema stills.
tools.avoid_when: Batch throughput or VRAM headroom matters.
tools.notes: DRCT (MIT) is a more efficient HAT-class rival.

tools.name: AuraSR v2
tools.license: v2 weights Apache-2.0; v1 repo CC-BY-SA-4.0
tools.ai_gen_note: The one deterministic tool built for generated images. Use the overlapped method to avoid tile seams (~2x time). Can over-invent on faces.
tools.use_when: AI-generated input, max fast detail, commercial-clean (v2 Apache).
tools.avoid_when: Strict facial fidelity / no fabricated detail.
tools.notes: A 2024 release, not 2026. No v3 found.

tools.name: Topaz Gigapixel
tools.ai_gen_note: Best non-generative still detail; generative models (Redefine/Bloom) hallucinate and drift identity. Per-frame, flickers on sequences.
tools.use_when: Single AI frames as stills (poster, key art, storyboard); non-generative firm-up of soft 720p.
tools.avoid_when: Moving sequences (use Topaz Video); face fidelity across frames with generative models.
tools.notes: Also a metered API ($0.08-0.12/credit, 24 MP/credit). 'Lines' model in the brief not found; likely Text & Shapes.

tools.name: Magnific (Precision / Creative)
tools.ai_gen_note: Best at reinventing detail; Precision mode is the AI-gen-safe default; Creative high-slider drifts identity across frames.
tools.use_when: Finishing a hero still/poster from an AI generation; Precision for faithful, Creative for reinvention.
tools.avoid_when: Batch video frames (cost + flicker + inconsistency), or guaranteed fidelity to source.
tools.notes: Consolidated under Magnific brand ~Apr 2026. Standalone vs in-Freepik pricing conflicts ~5x across sources; re-pull.

tools.name: fal.ai SeedVR2 (image)
tools.ai_gen_note: Clean, slightly AI-styled, very cheap; best $/quality for synthetic stills and doubles as a frame processor.
tools.use_when: Cheap volume stills; the standout hosted value.
tools.avoid_when: Temporal video coherence (per-frame) or guaranteed-fidelity restore.
tools.notes: Cheapest sane-quality hosted image path by a wide margin.

tools.name: Recraft Crisp Upscale
tools.ai_gen_note: Deterministic, won't amplify hallucination; clean cheap pass on AI stills.
tools.use_when: Dirt-cheap deterministic 4x crisp pass on AI stills.
tools.avoid_when: >4x or maximum perceived detail (Magnific/Topaz win).
tools.notes: One of the cheapest deterministic hosted options.

tools.name: NVIDIA RTX Video Super Resolution (ComfyUI node)
tools.ai_gen_note: Shipped Feb 2026, Blackwell-native, markets exactly the 720p->4K AI-video case; NVFP4 cuts VRAM ~60%. Per-frame class, verify quality on synthetic faces.
tools.use_when: Free fast 4K on the 5090; first thing to test for the cheap-fast corner.
tools.avoid_when: Max detail rebuild or temporal-critical work (verify per-frame on synthetic input).
tools.notes: Surfaced by the verification/critic pass; not in the original scout slices.
